policy.title: RBI Issues Final Guidelines on Climate Risk Management for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI released final guidelines mandating climate scenario analysis and stress testing for all scheduled commercial banks with assets over ₹50,000 crore, effective April 2027. Banks must integrate physical and transition risk metrics into ICAAP submissions and disclose portfolio-level climate VaR.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Include Scope 3 Emissions for Top 250 Listed Companies
policy.description: SEBI amended BRSR Core framework requiring top 250 companies by market cap to report Scope 3 emissions across eight material categories starting FY2027. Third-party assurance requirements strengthened with mandatory reasonable assurance on climate metrics.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI Issues Exposure Draft on Index-Based Insurance Products for Climate Perils
policy.description: IRDAI published exposure draft allowing general insurers to offer parametric products for cyclone, flood, and heat stress events without traditional loss adjustment. Draft specifies minimum trigger transparency requirements and mandates third-party index verification.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee Finalizes Pillar 3 Climate Risk Disclosure Framework
policy.description: BCBS published final Pillar 3 disclosure requirements for climate-related financial risks, establishing standardized templates for transition risk metrics and physical risk exposures. Implementation phased from January 2027 for G-SIBs, January 2028 for other internationally active banks.

policy.title: MCA Announces Phased ISSB Alignment Roadmap for Indian Companies
policy.description: Ministry of Corporate Affairs released roadmap for IFRS S1 and S2 adoption, with voluntary compliance from FY2027 and mandatory adoption for Nifty 500 companies from FY2029. Indian Sustainability Standards Board to issue India-specific guidance by December 2026.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Granularity
policy.description: NGFS published updated scenario set with improved regional granularity for South Asia and new chronic physical risk variables including water stress and agricultural productivity. Scenarios now extend to 2100 with five-year intervals for transition pathway modeling.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for India Physical Risk Analytics
competitors.description: Jupiter Intelligence announced strategic partnership with HDFC Bank to provide asset-level physical risk scores across the bank's corporate and retail mortgage portfolios. Partnership marks Jupiter's first major deployment with an Indian financial institution.

competitors.title: Sprih Raises $18M Series B to Expand BRSR Compliance Platform
competitors.description: Bengaluru-based Sprih closed $18 million Series B led by Nexus Venture Partners to scale its automated ESG data collection and BRSR reporting platform. Company claims 120 enterprise clients including 15 Nifty 100 companies.

competitors.title: Moody's Launches Climate-Adjusted PD Model for Emerging Market Banks
competitors.description: Moody's ESG Solutions released climate-adjusted probability of default model incorporating physical and transition risk factors for emerging market corporate credit. Model covers 15,000 issuers across India, Brazil, and Southeast Asia with sector-specific stress multipliers.

competitors.title: XDI Expands Cross Dependency Initiative Database to Cover Indian Commercial Real Estate
competitors.description: Australian climate risk firm XDI added comprehensive physical risk ratings for 450,000 Indian commercial properties across 50 cities. Database includes asset-level scores for flooding, cyclone, and extreme heat exposure with RCP 4.5 and 8.5 projections.

competitors.title: Watershed Partners with Tata Group for Enterprise Carbon Accounting
competitors.description: San Francisco-based Watershed announced multi-year partnership with Tata Sons to deploy carbon accounting infrastructure across Tata Group subsidiaries. Deal positions Watershed as primary decarbonization platform for one of India's largest conglomerates.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory Launches Natural Capital Risk Assessment for Indian Banks
competitors.description: GIST Advisory released natural capital and biodiversity risk screening tool designed for Indian lenders, covering deforestation exposure and water dependency metrics for agricultural and manufacturing portfolios. Tool aligned with TNFD framework requirements.

research.title: Climate Risk and Indian Banking: Estimating Physical Risk Impacts on Asset Quality
research.description: RBI study finds that a 2°C warming scenario could increase NPAs in agriculture-exposed portfolios by 8-12% by 2035, with coastal states showing highest vulnerability. Report recommends integrating district-level hazard data into credit risk frameworks.

research.title: Parametric Insurance in Emerging Markets: Scaling Climate Risk Transfer
research.description: Swiss Re analysis projects parametric insurance premiums in Asia-Pacific to reach $4.2 billion by 2030, with India identified as highest growth market. Report highlights basis risk management and index credibility as key adoption barriers requiring technological solutions.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Sustainable Investment
research.description: IFC report finds that 65% of mid-cap companies in South Asia lack adequate climate disclosures, creating $180 billion annual investment gap due to data unavailability. Report calls for alternative data approaches and regulatory harmonization across emerging markets.

research.title: Integrating Climate Risk into Credit Risk Modeling: A Practical Framework
research.description: UNEP FI working group published methodology for incorporating physical hazard data into PD and LGD models, with case studies from five pilot banks including ICICI Bank. Framework recommends sector-specific climate sensitivity factors and forward-looking scenario overlays.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk Assessment for Financial Institutions
research.description: NGFS technical document provides guidance on translating climate hazard data into financial risk metrics, emphasizing need for asset-level geolocation and sector vulnerability mapping. Document includes reference implementation for emerging market banking supervisors.

research.title: Catastrophe Risk Financing in South Asia: Innovation and Scale
research.description: World Bank assessment finds India's protection gap for climate perils exceeds $35 billion annually, with parametric structures offering 40% faster payout times than indemnity products. Report recommends regulatory frameworks enabling bank-insurance partnerships for distribution.

ourRead.headline: RBI's final climate guidelines create a ₹50,000 crore compliance mandate that positions StepChange's PD/LGD integration as essential infrastructure for Indian banks.
body: This week's signals converge on a single theme: Indian financial regulators are moving from aspiration to enforcement on climate risk, while global competitors are racing to establish India footholds before compliance deadlines hit. The window for StepChange to lock in strategic bank relationships is narrowing but still open.

The RBI's final climate risk guidelines (policy-1) are the week's most consequential development. By mandating climate scenario analysis and portfolio-level climate VaR for banks above ₹50,000 crore, RBI has created a compliance requirement that directly maps to StepChange's core PD/LGD offering. The April 2027 deadline gives us 12 months to become embedded in bank risk infrastructure. The companion RBI research report (research-1) quantifying 8-12% NPA increases in agriculture portfolios provides the empirical ammunition our sales conversations need.

Jupiter's HDFC partnership (comp-1) and Moody's emerging market PD model launch (comp-3) signal that global players are prioritizing India. Jupiter entering through India's largest private bank is a direct competitive threat to our Bet 1. However, both global entrants lack the regulatory context and local hazard calibration that RBI's framework demands. Our advantage lies in building products that speak directly to RBI's ICAAP requirements rather than retrofitting global models. The Sprih fundraise (comp-2) shows investor appetite for India climate-finance infrastructure remains strong.

The IFC report on ESG data gaps (research-3) quantifying a $180 billion investment gap due to disclosure failures validates our Bet 3 thesis entirely. Combined with IRDAI's parametric exposure draft (policy-3) enabling index-based climate products, there's a clear path to bundling our hazard intelligence with parametric product design for banks seeking fee income from climate risk transfer.

Watch: IRDAI's comment period on parametric guidelines closes in 45 days—StepChange should submit formal comments positioning our index verification capabilities. Action: Schedule briefings with risk heads at three banks in the ₹50,000-100,000 crore asset range this month, using the RBI research findings as the conversation opener.
